comparator: llama.cpp acd79d603 (Windows CPU-only build) /completion, greedy (temp 0/top_k 1/seed 0), -ngl 0 -ctk f32 -ctv f32 -fa off --no-repack -c 8704
camelid_backend: q8_0 runtime, RTX 3060 Laptop 6 GiB: ALL five buckets fully GPU-resident — the 0.6B weights (639 MiB) leave a resident KV cap of 36297 positions covering every bucket. No CPU-fallback.
execution_note: Single-model runs only: the llama.cpp oracle was captured alone and its server killed before camelid was started (engines never co-resident).
parity_semantics: ENGINE-CORRECTNESS parity: camelid reproduces the pinned llama.cpp greedy output token-for-token at each promoted context length. The tiny 0.6B model degenerates into repetitive/hallucinated output at longer contexts (e.g. looping "Answer:\nCMLD-<n>"); this bundle does NOT claim the 0.6B's generated TEXT is correct or useful — only that camelid's decode of this exact model matches the reference bit-for-bit.

buckets.note: NOT identical — benign greedy near-tie (see near_tie); not claimed

near_tie.description: At generation index 12 (both engines already in a degenerate loop on this tiny model) camelid's top token is 'The' (logprob -1.40) and the oracle's is 'Answer' (-1.50) — a ~0.10-nat near-tie. A benign greedy coin-flip between two degenerate continuations, not a camelid decode error; but not token-identical, so 1024 is NOT promoted. Note the ADJACENT longer buckets 2048/4096/8192 ARE token-identical (camelid matches the oracle's degenerate output exactly there), so this is a single isolated near-tie, not a context-length failure.

claim_boundary: PROMOTED (non-contiguous): bounded 512/2048/4096/8192-context (446/2013/4088/8253 Qwen3 tokens) raw-decode greedy parity for the exact Qwen3-0.6B-Q8_0 row ONLY — camelid fully-GPU-resident CUDA decode is token-AND-text-identical to the pinned llama.cpp acd79d603 oracle at 50 generated tokens on these four buckets. NOT PROMOTED: the 1024 bucket, which diverges at a documented benign ~0.10-nat greedy near-tie (the two adjacent longer buckets pass, so it is an isolated flip, not a context failure). This is ENGINE-CORRECTNESS parity (camelid == reference), NOT a claim about the 0.6B's text quality (the tiny model degenerates at context). NOT claimed: the 1024 bucket, model-native/larger context beyond 8192, other Qwen3 sizes/quants, thinking-mode, or throughput.
